nodes.label: AI as time-saving tool
nodes.label_negative: AI as replacement for personal understanding
nodes.grounding_span: I do see myself using it more as a time-saving device for course planning

nodes.label: AI as writing assistant
nodes.label_negative: AI as replacement for student writing skills
nodes.grounding_span: While I think AI can be a useful writing helper, I also think it's important for students to have those skills for themselves

nodes.label: AI as tool for critical thinking about AI
nodes.label_negative: AI as replacement for critical thinking
nodes.grounding_span: students write a podcast script on the use of AI in higher ed. That's been a useful project to address the pros and cons

nodes.label: AI transparency and reproducibility
nodes.grounding_span: Showing students how important it is to ground information, and being able to review the sources used to synthesize information

nodes.label: AI as ethical boundary-respecting tool
nodes.grounding_span: if allow AI's work to go unvetted, that could lead to problems

nodes.label: AI as tool for understanding AI limitations
nodes.grounding_span: I've shown students how AI doesn't always get things right

nodes.label: Personal connection with students
nodes.grounding_span: I want to ensure that I understand what my students are doing rather than handing that all over to another entity

nodes.label: Student learning and skill development
nodes.grounding_span: I maintain reading and writing continue to be counted among them

nodes.label: Ethical and responsible AI use
nodes.grounding_span: how we can learn to ethically use it

nodes.label: Information verification and source transparency
nodes.grounding_span: helps to verify the information ourselves and show students the value in that skill

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for mentorship planning
nodes.grounding_span: the scheduling for the mentorship felt like a solid use of time and AI tools

nodes.label: Frustration with student AI misuse
nodes.grounding_span: I fear that too many students use it as a replacement for their own critical and analytical thinking

nodes.label: Openness to increasing AI use in future
nodes.grounding_span: I do see myself using it more as a time-saving device for course planning

nodes.label: Concern about AI's ethical risks
nodes.grounding_span: if allow AI's work to go unvetted, that could lead to problems

nodes.label: Hands-on experiential verification — redoing materials to maintain personal connection
nodes.grounding_span: I redo the worksheets myself each time to ensure I'm on the same page as my students

nodes.label: Boundary-setting and ethical deliberation
nodes.grounding_span: I find it unethical to use AI to do the work rather than students doing it themselves
